title: Tangy Irish Lamb Stew
description: A low-sodium, dense one-pot lamb stew with rose water and a bright citrus tang, featuring a sousvide tender core.
instructions: Season the lamb cubes with salt and rosemary, then vacuum‑seal them for sous‑vide.
Cook the lamb sous‑vide at 140°F (60°C) for 24 hours to achieve melt‑in‑your‑mouth tenderness.
Remove the lamb from the bag, pat dry, and sear in a hot skillet with olive oil until browned on all sides.
Transfer the lamb to a large pot and add diced onion, minced garlic, sliced carrots, and diced potatoes.
Stir in lemon zest, lemon juice, rose water, and low‑sodium chicken broth.
Bring to a gentle boil, then reduce to a simmer and cook uncovered for 30 minutes, or until vegetables are tender.
Slice the sous‑vide lamb into thin strips and return them to the pot.
Simmer for an additional 5 minutes to meld flavors, adjusting seasoning as needed.
Serve the stew hot, optionally garnished with fresh rosemary sprigs.
